productIdea: A maintenance request portal for homeowners associations that helps residents submit requests, board members triage and assign vendors, and everyone track status updates.
targetAudience: HOA residents, board members, and vendors.
problemStatement: Maintenance requests are lost, residents do not know status, and board members struggle to track vendor assignments and completion.
constraints: Keep the first release simple and local-first. Focus on request tracking and status updates.
desiredOutput: A build-ready markdown workspace with phased planning, request state definitions, vendor handoff rules, gates, verification files, handoff context, and enough clarity for an HOA maintenance portal.
mustHaveFeatures: Request submission, triage workflow, vendor assignment, status updates, resident notifications.
niceToHaveFeatures: Photo attachments, vendor ratings, recurring maintenance schedules, payment tracking.
dataAndIntegrations: Requests, residents, vendors, status history, assignment records.
risks: Requests may stall without resident visibility, vendor handoffs may lose context, and board members may be overwhelmed.
successMetrics: A reviewer can trace a request from submission through triage to vendor completion and resident notification.
nonGoals: No payment processing, no legal document storage, no resident voting in v1.
timeline: Plan a realistic first release that proves the request workflow works before adding richer features.
teamContext: An HOA board member uses Xelera Method to prepare the portal plan and then hands the workspace to Codex, Claude Code, OpenCode, or a development team for phased implementation.

questionnaireAnswers.north-star: The first release must prove that residents can submit requests, board members can triage and assign vendors, and residents can track status.
questionnaireAnswers.primary-workflow: A resident submits a maintenance request, a board member triages it, assigns a vendor, the vendor updates status, and the resident receives notifications at key stages.
questionnaireAnswers.scope-cut: Keep request submission, triage, vendor assignment, and status updates in v1. Defer payments, ratings, and recurring schedules.
questionnaireAnswers.acceptance: A reviewer should be able to trace request states, vendor handoff, and resident notification without hidden assumptions.
questionnaireAnswers.operating-risks: The main risks are request stalls, lost vendor context, and board member overwhelm.
questionnaireAnswers.user-segments: Primary users are residents submitting requests. Secondary users are board members triaging and vendors completing work.
questionnaireAnswers.stakeholder-workflow: Residents submit. Board triages and assigns. Vendors execute and update. Residents track.
